summary: Complete testing of Daily Status subordinate dropdown feature. Backend fix confirmed working - deps.py now correctly uses db.user_sessions instead of db.sessions. All 11 backend API tests pass. Frontend: Dropdown visible for CEO (11 subordinates), hidden for Partner (0 subordinates), read-only mode works when viewing subordinate status.
passed_tests: Backend: Login CEO returns session token and user data
Backend: Login Partner returns session token and user data
Backend: Login with invalid credentials returns 401
Backend: GET /api/users/subordinates/all with invalid token returns 401
Backend: GET /api/users/subordinates/all for CEO returns 10+ subordinates
Backend: GET /api/users/subordinates/all for Partner returns empty array []
Backend: GET /api/users/subordinates/all for Director returns subordinates list
Backend: Subordinates are sorted alphabetically by name
Backend: Session token works for multiple consecutive requests
Backend: Session token works via cookie as well as header
Frontend: Daily Status page loads successfully
Frontend: 'View Status For' dropdown visible for CEO with 11 subordinates
Frontend: 'Myself' option present in dropdown with user name
Frontend: Subordinates listed with name and role in dropdown
Frontend: Selecting subordinate shows read-only indicator message
Frontend: Form fields disabled when viewing subordinate status
Frontend: 'View Status For' section NOT visible for Partner (no subordinates)
Frontend: Submit button visible for users viewing their own status
critical_code_review_comments: DailyStatusUpdate.js correctly conditionally renders dropdown based on subordinates.length > 0 (line 575)
isViewingOwnStatus correctly determines editable vs read-only state (line 229)
Form fields properly disabled via disabled={!isViewingOwnStatus} pattern
context_for_next_testing_agent: Daily Status subordinate dropdown feature tested. Key data-testids: resource-selector, daily-status-page, date-yesterday, date-today, date-picker, fetch-activities-button, submit-status-button. API endpoint: GET /api/users/subordinates/all. The dropdown only appears for users with subordinates. deps.py was fixed to use db.user_sessions (was db.sessions causing 401 errors).

verified_features.Authentication: Session token via cookie and Authorization header both work
verified_features.Subordinates API: Returns all direct and indirect reports sorted by name
verified_features.Dropdown Visibility: Conditional rendering based on subordinates count
verified_features.Read-only Mode: Forms disabled when viewing subordinate's status

rca_of_the_issue: The P0 bug was caused by a database collection name mismatch in deps.py. Login endpoint stored sessions in 'user_sessions' collection, but get_current_user_from_cookie_or_header was looking in 'sessions' collection. Fix: Changed line 38 and 58 in deps.py from db.sessions to db.user_sessions.
